rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Insert component type annexes into the component type scope
Insert implementation annexes into the component implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope when writing AGREE contracts
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component structure as the input AADL model
Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope when writing AGREE contracts
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not use AADL property names directly as AGREE variables
